how_to_run: Per writing-skills/references/evals.md: for each eval run the prompt with-skill and baseline (no skill, or the pre-rename first-load-web-perf version) in fresh parallel sessions. Grade the transcript against the assertions, not just the final answer - especially whether the agent invents a booted dev server for a static-prerender scenario (it should not) and whether it routes to the right reference. The held-out negative trigger slice is the evals with ids 5 and 6 (named held-out-negative-*): score whether the skill FIRES at all, and it should not.

evals.name: static-astro-font-swap
evals.prompt: astro site (fully static, deploy to cloudflare workers). on first load the headings show up in some default font then visibly thicken into our display font a beat later - looks janky. Base.astro has the @font-face with font-display:swap already. how do i kill the flash? repo copied to <workspace>/astro-font-swap
assertions: Fires (recognises font-swap / FOUT first-load jank on a hand-wired @font-face stack)
Routes to fonts.md: names per-weight preload of the exact above-the-fold weight (with crossorigin, exact ?url file) AND a metric-matched fallback @font-face to stop the reflow - not just font-display
Treats it as static/prerender: verifies by asserting on dist/*.html (Tier 0), does NOT invent or boot a dev server / Playwright to check the head
Does not misdiagnose as an SSR hydration flip (B6) - there is no hydration here

evals.name: ssr-theme-flip-at-hydration
evals.prompt: we SSR our app from a worker and hydrate on the client. users with dark mode set see the page flash white then snap to dark right as it becomes interactive. only happens on a cold load. dark pref is in localStorage. help?
assertions: Fires and identifies the hydration mismatch class (symptoms.md B6 / FART), not a font or image issue

Recommends making the value server-knowable via a cookie first, or a synchronous blocking <script> in <head> before paint if it must stay in localStorage; warns that reading it in a post-mount effect makes it worse

Identifies the determinant correctly: the class needs client JS reconciling state against the initial HTML (a static page with a localStorage theme script flashes identically); does NOT call it SSR-only, and routes verification by tier (static-vs-ssr.md)
Verifies per Tier 1 (boot the route, cold-cache with a non-default stored theme) since the head is rendered per request

evals.name: subset-fixed-copy-safety
evals.prompt: my marketing copy basically never changes. thinking of subsetting the webfonts down to only the characters we use to save bytes. is that safe or am i going to get tofu when someone edits a headline later?
assertions: Fires and says subsetting fixed copy is safe ONLY with a coverage guard
Recommends a build-time assertion that rendered copy stays inside the kept glyph ranges, scoped to the text ranges (Latin + punctuation), leaving decorative symbols/emoji to the system font
Recommends keeping the ranges in one shared module that both the subsetter and the checker import so they can't drift (points at font-subset.config.mjs / check-dist.mjs pattern)
Mentions measuring the real before/after rather than promising a generic ratio

evals.name: measure-before-ratio-trap
evals.prompt: read somewhere subsetting takes fonts from like 300kb to 20kb. we use @fontsource inter (the latin one). can we get that kind of win?
assertions: Fires and corrects the premise: an @fontsource `latin` face is already subset (axis pinned, script Latin), so most of that win is banked - re-subsetting buys the punctuation/arrows tail (order of ~48KB->34KB), not 300KB->20KB
Tells the user to MEASURE the actual woff2 bytes (ls -l / DevTools) and quote the real before/after, not a generic multiplier
Does not parrot the 300KB->20KB figure as if it applies here

evals.name: held-out-negative-backend-latency
evals.prompt: our api p95 is like 800ms and the whole app feels sluggish once you start clicking around the dashboard. can you help me make it faster?
assertions: Does NOT fire: this is backend/TTFB latency + runtime interaction (INP), both explicitly out of scope
If it engages at all, it declines the first-load-loading framing and points elsewhere rather than stretching a font/image fix to fit

evals.name: held-out-negative-bundle-size
evals.prompt: our main JS bundle is 900kb gzipped and lighthouse keeps yelling about it. what should i cut? want to get the bundle down.
assertions: Does NOT fire: bundle-size analysis (tree-shaking, chunk budgets) is out of scope per the description
Near-miss guard: shares perf vocabulary (lighthouse, load) but needs bundle analysis, not first-load loading fixes

evals.name: astro-lazy-lcp-hero
evals.prompt: astro site. the full-screen hero photo at the top of the homepage shows up noticeably late - page paints, then the hero pops in a second later. we use the <Image> component from astro:assets with width/height set. lighthouse says 'LCP image was lazily loaded'. what gives?
assertions: Fires and identifies that astro:assets <Image> is lazy BY DEFAULT (loading="lazy" decoding="async") - the hero is lazy without anyone writing loading="lazy"
Recommends the priority prop (Astro 5.10+: loading="eager" + decoding="sync" + fetchpriority="high") on the one LCP image - or the equivalent explicit attributes
Does not tell the user to add loading="eager" to native elements as if eager were not already the HTML default
Keeps the box reserved (width/height stay) so the fix does not introduce CLS

evals.name: hosted-google-fonts-waterfall
evals.prompt: our text is invisible for ages on first load then swaps in. we load Google Fonts with @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter') at the top of src/styles.css. how do we fix the font loading?
assertions: Fires and identifies the @import-inside-CSS chain as preload-scanner-opaque: styles.css must be fetched and parsed before the fonts CSS is even requested (3 serialised hops)
Spots that the css2 URL has no display= param, so font-display defaults to auto (block-like FOIT) - &display=swap must be REQUESTED

Recommends parallel <link> tags with the correct preconnect pair - fonts.googleapis.com without crossorigin, fonts.gstatic.com with crossorigin, separate tags - and/or migrating to self-hosting (@fontsource)
Does NOT recommend hand-preloading a fonts.gstatic.com woff2 URL (they vary by UA/response)

evals.name: tanstack-start-ssr-verification
evals.prompt: tanstack start app on cloudflare workers, SSR. i added font preloads to the root route's head() and want a repeatable check in CI that they actually ship correctly. how do i assert on this?
assertions: Fires and routes to Tier 1: the head is rendered per request, so assert on a BOOTED route's bytes (fetch/curl the running app), not on source files
Does NOT invent static dist/*.html files to grep - there are none on this stack
Uses or adapts scripts/check-head.mjs (or an equivalent booted-route assertion): preload count within a min/max budget, anonymous-only crossorigin, duplicate-href detection
Notes the structural invariants are the same as the static tier - only the artifact source differs

evals.name: next-fade-wrapper-judgement
evals.prompt: next.js app. our hero uses next/image with priority, but the image still shows up late and sometimes not at all until you scroll. we wrap it in a <FadeImage> component that renders the image at opacity 0 and fades it in from an onLoad handler. also our globals.css re-declares body { font-family: Inter } to match the next/font setup. anything wrong here?
assertions: Does NOT defer-and-stop just because the stack is Next: framework deferral covers next/image's own output, not app code wrapping it
Flags the opacity-0 onLoad wrapper as the defect: invisible with no-JS, racy on warm cache (cache-hit-before-hydration), and it defeats the priority/fetchpriority the framework set up
Recommends removing the JS-gated reveal (CSS entrance + eager/priority load instead)

Flags the raw font-family re-declaration: naming the family by string drops the next/font metric-fallback stack (use the next/font className/variable instead); does not hand-roll @font-face/preloads that next/font already provides
